plan.title: contracts-01: implement the first PRISM contracts primitive as a concept-like sibling pipeline with explicit schema and event storage, hosted MCP mutations and resources, query/read integration, and contract-aware impact or after-edit behavior, while keeping derived policy or guardrail enforcement out of scope for this first rollout.
plan.goal: contracts-01: implement the first PRISM contracts primitive as a concept-like sibling pipeline with explicit schema and event storage, hosted MCP mutations and resources, query/read integration, and contract-aware impact or after-edit behavior, while keeping derived policy or guardrail enforcement out of scope for this first rollout.

plan.goal: contracts-01: implement the first PRISM contracts primitive as a concept-like sibling pipeline with explicit schema and event storage, hosted MCP mutations and resources, query/read integration, and contract-aware impact or after-edit behavior, while keeping derived policy or guardrail enforcement out of scope for this first rollout.

node.title: Milestone 0: Lock the contracts V1 ontology and rollout boundaries
node.summary: Resolve the spec decisions that must be frozen before implementation: minimum contract fields, initial kind and status vocabularies, event operations, promotion scopes, subject anchoring rules, and the explicit out-of-scope list for contracts-01 so the first rollout does not absorb policies or broad inference.

acceptance.label: The contracts-01 rollout has an explicit V1 scope that includes schema or storage, read surfaces, mutations, and limited query integration but excludes derived policy enforcement
The minimum contract object and event model are narrow enough to ship without a large second taxonomy pass
Open questions that would block implementation are reduced to a small explicit list or folded into concrete V1 choices

plan.title: contracts-01: implement the first PRISM contracts primitive as a concept-like sibling pipeline with explicit schema and event storage, hosted MCP mutations and resources, query/read integration, and contract-aware impact or after-edit behavior, while keeping derived policy or guardrail enforcement out of scope for this first rollout.
plan.goal: contracts-01: implement the first PRISM contracts primitive as a concept-like sibling pipeline with explicit schema and event storage, hosted MCP mutations and resources, query/read integration, and contract-aware impact or after-edit behavior, while keeping derived policy or guardrail enforcement out of scope for this first rollout.

node.title: Milestone 1: Add contract IR, event storage, and projection loading
node.summary: Implement first-class contract types and append-only event storage with the same broad shape PRISM already uses for repo-published concepts: contract schema in prism-ir, repo event log and load or projection helpers in prism-core, and session or repo scope handling that keeps contracts as published knowledge rather than scratch notes.

acceptance.label: Contract types, enums, and event records exist as first-class IR rather than ad hoc JSON blobs
Repo-scoped contracts round-trip through a dedicated append-only contract event log and reload into live state
The implementation follows the existing modular knowledge pipeline shape instead of embedding core contract logic in facade files or coordination-only code

plan.title: contracts-01: implement the first PRISM contracts primitive as a concept-like sibling pipeline with explicit schema and event storage, hosted MCP mutations and resources, query/read integration, and contract-aware impact or after-edit behavior, while keeping derived policy or guardrail enforcement out of scope for this first rollout.
plan.goal: contracts-01: implement the first PRISM contracts primitive as a concept-like sibling pipeline with explicit schema and event storage, hosted MCP mutations and resources, query/read integration, and contract-aware impact or after-edit behavior, while keeping derived policy or guardrail enforcement out of scope for this first rollout.

node.title: Milestone 2: Expose contract mutations, schemas, and resource surfaces
node.summary: Extend the MCP host so agents can create and maintain contracts explicitly: add a contract mutation action with promote, update, retire, attach_evidence, attach_validation, and record_consumer operations, register contract-aware schemas and examples, and expose resource surfaces such as prism://contracts instead of forcing raw event-log reads.

acceptance.label: prism_mutate exposes explicit contract lifecycle operations with validated payloads and actionable schema examples
The MCP resource surface exposes inspectable contract state without reading raw event logs directly
Hosted contract mutations update the live runtime consistently with persisted contract state

plan.title: contracts-01: implement the first PRISM contracts primitive as a concept-like sibling pipeline with explicit schema and event storage, hosted MCP mutations and resources, query/read integration, and contract-aware impact or after-edit behavior, while keeping derived policy or guardrail enforcement out of scope for this first rollout.
plan.goal: contracts-01: implement the first PRISM contracts primitive as a concept-like sibling pipeline with explicit schema and event storage, hosted MCP mutations and resources, query/read integration, and contract-aware impact or after-edit behavior, while keeping derived policy or guardrail enforcement out of scope for this first rollout.

node.title: Milestone 3: Integrate contracts into normal query and review flows
node.summary: Make contracts alter agent-visible reads instead of acting as a passive metadata store: add query helpers and target or concept lookups, then thread contract awareness into impact and after-edit style surfaces so PRISM can name touched promises, known consumers, assumptions, and defending validations for changed targets.

acceptance.label: Agents can ask which contracts govern a target and inspect those contracts through standard read flows
At least one existing impact-oriented surface reports touched contracts, affected consumers, or contract-linked validations when relevant
The first integration distinguishes implementation-only edits from contract-affecting edits more clearly than the current baseline

plan.title: contracts-01: implement the first PRISM contracts primitive as a concept-like sibling pipeline with explicit schema and event storage, hosted MCP mutations and resources, query/read integration, and contract-aware impact or after-edit behavior, while keeping derived policy or guardrail enforcement out of scope for this first rollout.
plan.goal: contracts-01: implement the first PRISM contracts primitive as a concept-like sibling pipeline with explicit schema and event storage, hosted MCP mutations and resources, query/read integration, and contract-aware impact or after-edit behavior, while keeping derived policy or guardrail enforcement out of scope for this first rollout.

node.title: Milestone 4: Validate, dogfood, and restart the live MCP runtime
node.summary: Add focused tests across core, query, and MCP surfaces, dogfood the first contracts workflow on real repo targets, and then rebuild and restart the release MCP binaries so the live PRISM server reflects the new contract primitive during the same session.

acceptance.label: Core storage, query lookup, and MCP mutation or resource surfaces have targeted test coverage for the first contracts path
Dogfooding yields at least one recorded validation outcome or feedback item about the contracts rollout
After meaningful MCP or query-surface changes, the release binaries are rebuilt and the live MCP daemon is restarted and health-checked

node.title: Milestone 0: Lock the contracts V1 ontology and rollout boundaries
node.summary: Milestone 0 is now codified in the implementation: contracts landed as a concept-like sibling surface with a narrow V1 model centered on contract packets, append-only contract events, repo event publication, basic query-side resolution, and explicit deferral of MCP mutation/resource exposure and richer contract-aware impact integration to later milestones.

acceptance.label: The contracts-01 rollout has an explicit V1 scope that includes schema or storage, read surfaces, mutations, and limited query integration but excludes derived policy enforcement
The minimum contract object and event model are narrow enough to ship without a large second taxonomy pass

node.title: Milestone 1: Add contract IR, event storage, and projection loading
node.summary: Initial Milestone 1 slice landed: contract IR and event types exist, repo contract events round-trip through .prism/contracts/events.jsonl and reload into indexed sessions, and the projection/query runtime now carries curated contracts with basic handle/query lookup. Remaining work in this milestone is to harden storage semantics and decide whether session/local contracts need first-class persisted projection support before MCP mutation surfaces build on top.

acceptance.label: Contract types, enums, and event records exist as first-class IR rather than ad hoc JSON blobs
Repo-scoped contracts round-trip through a dedicated append-only contract event log and reload into live state
The implementation follows the existing modular knowledge pipeline shape instead of embedding core contract logic in facade files or coordination-only code

node.summary: Milestone 3 is in progress: contracts now affect impact, afterEdit, validationPlan, readContext, query helpers, and prism_workset; review-specific contract guidance remains.
